name: Suspicious WMIC Execution
notes:
This rule detects WMIC executing suspicious or recon commands.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Michael Haag, Florian Roth, juju4, oscd.community
SIGMA Title: Suspicious WMIC Execution
SIGMA Description: Detects WMIC executing suspicious or recon commands


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success, Exploit.Command Execution

when the event matches (lower("command") matches '.*?wmic.*' and LOWER("Command") LIKE '%process%' and LOWER("Command") LIKE '%call%' and LOWER("Command") LIKE '%create %') or (LOWER("Command") LIKE '%wmic%' and LOWER("Command") LIKE '% path %' and (LOWER("Command") LIKE '%antivirus%' or LOWER("Command") LIKE '%firewall%') and LOWER("Command") LIKE '%product%' and LOWER("Command") LIKE '% get %') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious WMIC Execution on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success, Exploit.Command Execution

AND when the event matches (lower("command") matches '.*?wmic.*' and LOWER("Command") LIKE '%process%' and LOWER("Command") LIKE '%call%' and LOWER("Command") LIKE '%create %') or (LOWER("Command") LIKE '%wmic%' and LOWER("Command") LIKE '% path %' and (LOWER("Command") LIKE '%antivirus%' or LOWER("Command") LIKE '%firewall%') and LOWER("Command") LIKE '%product%' and LOWER("Command") LIKE '% get %') AQL filter query
RED_list: Event description: Detects WMIC executing suspicious or recon commands
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
